item.title: Finma Says Immediate Mythos Access Would Pose Systemic Bank Risk
item.description: Switzerland’s top financial regulator said giving banks quick and easy access to Anthropic PBC’s artificial intelligence tool Mythos would create a severe risk for the country’s financial system.

item.title: DOJ Probe Into Powell and Fed Building Costs Has Been Dropped
item.description: US Attorney General for DC Jeanine Pirro directs her office to close its investigation of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell as the inspector general for the agency has been asked to scrutinize its building construction project costs, according to a post on X.

item.title: US Consumer Sentiment Falls to Record Low on Inflation
item.description: US consumer sentiment fell in April from a month earlier to a record low, reflecting worries around the economic fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: Hedge Fund Launched by Instacart Co-Founder Has AI Call Shots
item.description: Instacart co-founder Apoorva Mehta launched a hedge fund that relies on an army of artificial intelligence agents, one of the few using that technology to essentially replace fundamental portfolio managers.

item.title: Saudi Arabia’s Hormuz Oil Bypass Is Yet to Maintain Full Exports
item.description: Saudi Arabia has achieved a huge boost in crude exports through its Red Sea terminals, but has yet to stabilize flows at its target level for the route.

item.title: Data Center Firm Csquare Files Confidentially for US IPO
item.description: Csquare filed confidentially for a US initial public offering, a signal that more data center companies are lining up to sell shares for the first time over the coming year.

item.title: Texas Ranch Lures Futuristic Startups to Revive US Manufacturing
item.description: The charismatic founders preach a gospel of reviving American manufacturing through frontier grit.

item.title: Intel to Hold Fixed-Income Calls Following Blowout Sales Outlook
item.description: Intel Corp. is set to hold fixed-income investor calls Friday after delivering a sales forecast that shattered Wall Street expectations.

item.title: Nasdaq Futures Jump as Intel’s Blockbuster Sales View Impresses
item.description: Nasdaq 100 Index futures jumped on Friday as a blockbuster sales forecast from Intel Corp. fueled a rally in technology stocks.

item.title: Cava Gets Lone Sell Rating After Fast-Casual Chain’s 123% Rally
item.description: A rally that’s more than doubled shares of Mediterranean fast-casual chain Cava Group Inc. in five months has gone too far, according to its new lone bear on Wall Street.

item.title: Canadian Consumer Spending Rises for Third Straight Month
item.description: Canadian consumer spending rebounded during the first quarter even as US tariffs and geopolitical tensions loomed over the economy.

item.title: India Mulls Options on Iran Port Stake Before Sanctions Kick In
item.description: India is considering various options regarding its stake in the Chabahar port in Iran, including temporarily divesting from it, ahead of the expiry of a US sanctions waiver in coming days, officials familiar with the matter said.

item.title: McKinsey Partner’s Drone Startup Stake Spurs Conflict Concerns
item.description: A top McKinsey & Co. consultant who advises on defense projects around the world has for years owned a personal stake in German military tech startup Helsing.

item.title: The Shape of a Deal to Come
item.description: Warsh needed to be asked about the Treasury takeover of monetary policy.

item.title: Meta Inks Multibillion-Dollar Deal to Use Amazon Chips for AI
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. and Meta Platforms Inc. have struck a multibillion-dollar deal for the social-media giant to rent hundreds of thousands of Amazon’s general-purpose chips for its AI efforts.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for April 24, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are up 0.4% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York as traders weigh corporate earnings and the US-Iran standoff.

item.title: ‘Bubble’ Signal Flagged by BofA as Tech Stocks, Volatility Soar
item.description: A record-smashing rally in technology megacaps has rekindled bubble warnings at Bank of America Corp.

item.title: Hedge Fund at Center of Avis Squeeze Added to Stake Before Rout
item.description: Avis Budget Group Inc.’s second-biggest shareholder Pentwater Capital Management LP boosted its stake in the car rental company just before the stock’s nearly 70% rout.

item.title: Oil Declines as Officials in Pakistan Say US-Iran Talks Expected
item.description: Oil futures declined, erasing earlier gains, as officials in Pakistan said a second round of US-Iran talks was expected.

item.title: Europe’s Growing Jet Fuel Problem Sends Airlines Into a Spin
item.description: The continent has lost about 20% of its usual supplies due to the war in Iran.

item.title: Europe’s Attempt to Protect Children Online Crashes into Its Privacy Laws
item.description: The region tries to balance the rights of users online with keeping children safe

item.title: Oil-Price Surge Fails to Stoke US Energy-Sector Enthusiasm
item.description: High crude prices are yet sparking enthusiasm among Texas oil producers.

item.title: Investors Bet on Trade Schools as AI Clouds White-Collar Job Market
item.description: Growing concerns about a white-collar job apocalypse are helping to turn once-sleepy trade school stocks into investor darlings.

item.title: Richer European Countries Question Multi-Trillion Budget Talks
item.description: Tensions over the European Union’s next long-term budget surfaced in negotiations among member states, with the bloc’s richer members insisting that their contributions to the common budget be reduced.

item.title: Europe’s Aerospace Stocks Rack Up Biggest Weekly Losses in Years
item.description: European aerospace stocks are suffering some of their biggest weekly losses in years on concerns that fuel prices will stay high due to the Iran war, curbing air travel demand and eroding profits.

item.title: Russia Cuts Key Rate With Eye on Iran War and Budget Risks
item.description: Russia’s central bank continued lowering borrowing costs, delivering some relief to an economy that’s losing steam while treading cautiously due to uncertainty around the budget and the war in the Middle East.

item.title: The Hottest Phone for Kids Right Now Is a $100 Landline
item.description: The Tin Can has gone viral over the past year, mostly through word of mouth. Now schools want to get even more of them in students' homes.

item.title: Tech Bulls Are Taking Charge of the Stock Market
item.description: Nasdaq 100 is leading the rebound from the March lows.

item.title: Petro Speeds $7 Billion Pension Transfers, Risking Market Shock
item.description: Colombia’s government is pressing ahead with a plan to transfer about $7 billion from private pension savings into the public retirement system, a move that risks destabilizing local markets as funds may be forced to rapidly liquidate assets to comply.

item.title: War Squeezes Global Mining as Diesel and Acid Supplies Tighten
item.description: From the Australian outback to Ethiopia and the Democratic Republic of Congo, the global mining industry is beginning to feel the effects of disruption caused by the war in Iran.
